display.purpose: Masthead title only. Italic EB Garamond at 3.2rem. The document's one typographic flourish.

display-zh.purpose: Masthead Chinese title. 700-weight Noto Serif TC at 2.2rem, sized smaller than English per the source-language hierarchy.

headline.purpose: Section and chapter titles within the body. Italic EB Garamond, 1.7rem.

title.purpose: Bilingual subhead rows across both columns. 1.25rem italic Garamond; cardinal-colored on English, plain Ming-style on Chinese.

body.purpose: English column body type. 18px EB Garamond, line-height 1.65. Justified with hyphens-auto. Line length ~70ch within the bilingual grid.

body-zh.purpose: Chinese column body type. 17.5px Noto Serif TC, line-height 1.95. Justified with inter-ideograph spacing.

label.purpose: All chrome labels. Small-caps EB Garamond at 0.78rem with 0.22em tracking, cardinal-colored.

shadows.name: popover-rest
shadows.purpose: The system's single resting shadow. Renders only on the scripture popover, never on cards or chrome.

motion.name: hover-color
motion.purpose: The only motion in the system. Used for link / scripture / footnote hover transitions. Disabled by prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Translator's Note
components.description: Above-the-fold bilingual disclaimer panel with leading section mark. Replaces what would otherwise be a forbidden side-stripe border with a § glyph at top-left.
components.html:
Translator’s Note & Source譯者按與來源

This edition places the official Vatican English text alongside an unofficial, AI‑assisted Traditional Chinese study translation. The English text is the authoritative original.

本版以梵蒂岡英文官方本，並列呈現一份非官方、由人工智能輔助製作的繁體中文研讀翻譯。英文本為權威原文。
components.css: .ds-translators-note { max-width: 70rem; margin: 1.75rem auto 0; border: 1px solid #d9cfb6; background: #f5efe0; padding: 1.25rem 1.5rem 1.35rem; border-radius: 4px; font-family: 'EB Garamond', 'Source Serif Pro', serif; font-size: 0.9rem; line-height: 1.6; color: #1c1916; position: relative; } .ds-translators-note::before { content: '§'; position: absolute; top: 1.1rem; left: 1.5rem; color: #8b1a1f; font-size: 1.4rem; line-height: 1; font-style: italic; } .ds-tn-label { font-variant: small-caps; letter-spacing: 0.22em; color: #8b1a1f; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.78rem; margin: 0 0 0.85rem 2rem; display: flex; gap: 0.75rem; align-items: baseline; } .ds-tn-label .zh { font-variant: normal; letter-spacing: 0.05em; font-family: 'Noto Serif TC', serif; font-weight: 700; } .ds-tn-cols { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 1.75rem; } .ds-tn-zh { font-family: 'Noto Serif TC', serif; font-size: 0.92rem; line-height: 1.8; } .ds-translators-note p { margin: 0; } .ds-translators-note strong { color: #1c1916; }

components.name: Bilingual Paragraph Row
components.description: A numbered paragraph row in the parallel-text bilingual grid. The Loeb / classical-edition convention: gold paragraph number in tracked small-caps in the left gutter, body text justified beside it, both columns aligned across.
components.html:
¶ 1
Humanity, created by God in all its grandeur, is today facing a pivotal choice: to construct a new Tower of Babel, or to build the city in which God and humanity dwell together.
¶ 1
天主在祂的全部壯麗中所創造的人類，今日正面臨一個關鍵的抉擇：是要興建一座新的「巴貝耳塔」，還是要建設一座天主與人共居的城。
components.css: .ds-bilingual { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr; gap: 2.5rem; font-family: 'EB Garamond', 'Source Serif Pro', serif; color: #1c1916; } .ds-col-zh { font-family: 'Noto Serif TC', serif; font-size: 17.5px; line-height: 1.95; } .ds-col:not(.ds-col-zh) { font-size: 18px; line-height: 1.65; } .ds-para { display: grid; grid-template-columns: 2.5rem 1fr; gap: 0.5rem; align-items: baseline; } .ds-num { text-align: right; color: #a07835; font-size: 0.82rem; padding-top: 0.4rem; font-variant: small-caps; letter-spacing: 0.05em; user-select: none; } .ds-body { text-align: justify; hyphens: auto; } .ds-col-zh .ds-body { text-justify: inter-ideograph; }

components.name: Scripture Link
components.description: Inline scripture citation anchor. Dotted gold underline plus a low-opacity yellow highlight wash under the bottom 30% of the text. Hover and focus shift to Magisterial Cardinal.
components.html: A skilled craftsman at His side (cf. Prov 8:22-31), rejoicing always in His presence.
components.css: .ds-scripture { text-decoration: none; border-bottom: 1px dotted #a07835; color: #1c1916; cursor: pointer; padding: 0 1px; background: linear-gradient(transparent 70%, rgba(160,120,53,0.18) 30%); transition: color 150ms ease-out, border-color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-scripture:hover { color: #8b1a1f; border-bottom-color: #8b1a1f; } .ds-scripture:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #8b1a1f; outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Footnote Reference
components.description: Superscript anchor marking a footnote. Default: cardinal, no underline. Hover: underline. Focus-visible: cardinal outline.
components.html: It is “only in the mystery of the Word made flesh that the mystery of humanity truly becomes clear.”[1]
components.css: .ds-fnref { font-size: 0.7em; vertical-align: super; text-decoration: none; color: #8b1a1f; padding: 0 1px; transition: color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-fnref:hover { text-decoration: underline; } .ds-fnref:focus-visible { outline: 1.5px solid #8b1a1f; outline-offset: 2px; border-radius: 2px; }

components.name: Section Label
components.description: The system's chrome label primitive: small-caps tracked Garamond in Magisterial Cardinal, with a bilingual partner in a 700-weight Chinese Ming-style face. Used for TOC headings, translator's-note label, section dividers, colophon labels.
components.html: Translator’s Note & Source譯者按與來源
components.css: .ds-label { font-variant: small-caps; letter-spacing: 0.22em; color: #8b1a1f; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.78rem; font-family: 'EB Garamond', serif; display: flex; gap: 0.75rem; align-items: baseline; } .ds-label .zh { font-variant: normal; letter-spacing: 0.05em; font-family: 'Noto Serif TC', serif; font-weight: 700; }

components.name: Scripture Popover
components.description: The system's only shadowed surface. Renders BSB English and 思高聖經 Chinese passages with bilingual cardinal-labeled headers.
components.html:
Prov 8:22-31 · BSB (English)
The LORD created me as His first course, before His works of old…
箴 8:22-31 · 思高聖經
上主自始即拿我作他行動的起始，作他作為的開端：
components.css: .ds-popover { max-width: 32rem; background: #fbf7ec; border: 1px solid #d9cfb6; box-shadow: 0 8px 30px rgba(28, 25, 22, 0.18); border-radius: 4px; padding: 1rem 1.1rem; font-family: 'EB Garamond', serif; font-size: 0.92rem; line-height: 1.55; color: #1c1916; position: relative; } .ds-popover-close { position: absolute; top: 0.4rem; right: 0.6rem; color: #7b7060; background: none; border: none; font-size: 1.2rem; cursor: pointer; line-height: 1; transition: color 150ms ease-out; } .ds-popover-close:hover { color: #8b1a1f; } .ds-popover-header { font-variant: small-caps; letter-spacing: 0.15em; color: #8b1a1f; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.85rem; margin-bottom: 0.35rem; } .ds-popover-en { margin-bottom: 0.75rem; font-style: italic; } .ds-popover-header-zh { font-family: 'Noto Serif TC', serif; font-variant: normal; font-weight: 700; color: #8b1a1f; font-size: 0.85rem; margin: 0.75rem 0 0.25rem; padding-top: 0.5rem; border-top: 1px dashed #d9cfb6; } .ds-popover-zh { font-family: 'Noto Serif TC', serif; }

narrative.northStar: The Loeb Edition for the AI Age
narrative.overview:
This is the visual system for a single document presented as a critical edition. The reference is a Loeb Classical Library volume, transplanted into 2026: parallel bilingual text (English / Traditional Chinese) as the entire reading surface, generous typography set in EB Garamond and Noto Serif TC, paragraph numbering, footnote apparatus, and interactive scripture cross-references that resolve in both languages. The apparatus is the design; decoration is what the apparatus doesn't need.

The palette is a warm-cream paper with a single magisterial cardinal accent, plus manuscript gold for paragraph numbers and rule lines. Surfaces are flat. Motion is essentially absent. Layout is the strict two-column bilingual grid that the Loeb format made famous, the same one Vatican Polyglot Press editions have used for centuries.

What this system explicitly rejects: AI/SaaS landing-page aesthetics, generic Wikipedia/Project Gutenberg plain-text indifference, megachurch web stock-photo warmth, and the currently-saturated Notion/Substack editorial-typographic clone. The lineage we are in (Loeb, Vatican Polyglot) is the original this trend imitates; the design should read as the source, not the copy.
keyCharacteristics: Bilingual two-column parallel-text grid is the entire UX
Cream paper background, never pure white
A single magisterial cardinal accent, used sparingly on labels, links, focus rings, and the leading section mark
Manuscript-gold paragraph numbers in tracked small-caps, in the left gutter
No buttons, no forms, no cards, no shadows except one popover
No motion beyond hover color changes; prefers-reduced-motion strips even those

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Magisterial Cardinal (#8b1a1f) is the document's only true accent. It carries labels, focus indicators, footnote markers, and hover state. It is never a background fill behind type. It does not appear on body type at rest. Its rarity is what makes the cardinal feel like a voice, not a decoration.

rules.name: The No-Pure-White Rule
rules.body: The page background is #fbf7ec, never #fff. Surfaces are #f5efe0, never #fafafa. The cream IS the paper; pure white would read as a screen and break the Loeb illusion immediately.

rules.name: The Cardinal-Never-Fills Rule
rules.body: Cardinal renders as type, border, outline, or 1px-icon stroke. It is never a background-color fill, never a button surface, never a callout body.

rules.name: The Italic-Garamond-Only-At-Scale Rule
rules.body: Italic Garamond is the document's headline gesture, used at 1.7rem and above. It is not used for body emphasis; the italic-at-scale is a Loeb / classical-edition convention; italic-at-body would be magazine.

rules.name: The No-Sans-Serif Rule
rules.body: This is a book. There is no sans-serif font on the page, ever, including labels and metadata. Adding a single sans would announce 'this is a website.'

rules.name: The Bilingual-Parity Rule
rules.body: Whatever the English column gets, the Chinese column gets. No language is a translation appendage of the other.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Resting surfaces are flat. The popover is the only shadowed surface in the system. If you find yourself wanting a shadow to separate one block from another, use the rule line or a tonal shift to paper-deep instead.

rules.name: The Tonal-Layer Rule
rules.body: The system has exactly three tonal layers: page, chrome, popover. No additional surface shades.

dos: Do preserve the bilingual two-column grid at every level (body, TOC, translator's note, popover).
Do use Magisterial Cardinal (#8b1a1f) only as type, border, outline, or 1px stroke. Never as a fill.
Do keep the paragraph-number gutter at the left of each column (the Loeb convention).
Do use middle-dots (·) as separators in chrome (TOC, labels, masthead). Both English and Chinese halves of the same line should use the same separator.
Do name colors with the manuscript voice for accents and the restrained voice for neutrals.
Do verify color contrast for Muted Stone on paper-deep whenever you scale down.
Do ship a print stylesheet whenever new chrome is added; encyclicals get printed for study.
Do keep visible :focus-visible outlines on every interactive element in Magisterial Cardinal at 1.5–2px width.
donts: Don't use side-stripe borders. border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent is forbidden by impeccable's absolute bans.

Don't use em dashes ( — or --) in chrome (UI copy, TOC, labels, page title, popover headers). Use commas, colons, semicolons, periods, or middle-dots. Chinese —— (full-width double dash) is preserved as Chinese typographic convention.
Don't add Cormorant or Cormorant Garamond to font stacks; both are on impeccable's reflex-reject list.
Don't introduce a sans-serif anywhere on the document. This is a book.
Don't use #000 or #fff. The page is cream paper with warm-black ink.
Don't drift into AI/SaaS landing-page aesthetics: gradients, hero CTAs, three-column feature cards, Inter or Plus Jakarta Sans.
Don't drift into megachurch / evangelical web style: stock photos of crowds and skies, casual sans, soft pastel backgrounds.
Don't drift into generic Wikipedia / Project Gutenberg plain-text indifference.
Don't add cards. The TOC and the translator's note are bordered note panels, not cards.
Don't add modals. The popover is positioned under the scripture link, not a modal.
Don't repeat the source attribution in both the translator's note and the colophon.
